title: Polymorphism, Protocols, and Abstract Contracts · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Support interchangeable storage, notification, pricing, and item-policy implementations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a protocol-driven circulation application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use behavior-based polymorphism where callers depend on supported operations rather than concrete class identity.
Define abstract base classes with required operations and optional shared behavior when nominal hierarchy is justified.
Define structural Protocol roles that match consumer needs and support static checking without forced inheritance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
